rationale: The citing paper explicitly states it "connects intermediary and transaction-decoupling theories" and proposes a mechanism where vouchers decouple purchase and reward, which directly aligns with the cited paper’s core construct of transaction decoupling. The cited work is a foundational theory/mechanism source on how bundling decouples costs and benefits, making this more than an incidental background mention. The DOI-matched reference (10.1509/jmkr.38.1.30.18828) supports that this specific source is being invoked.
